title: HEARTLAND Protocol FHIR Implementation Guide
description: FHIR R4 Implementation Guide defining interoperability resources for the HEARTLAND Protocol v3.2 — risk assessment, care plans, remote monitoring observations, questionnaires, value sets, and code systems — enabling EHR vendors and rural heart failure programs to exchange HEARTLAND-structured clinical data using US healthcare interoperability standards. Built with FHIR Shorthand (FSH) compiled via SUSHI; published as a navigable HTML site via the HL7 IG Publisher. Depends on US Core 6.1.0. Contents: 4 CodeSystems (risk tier, implementation tier, monitoring track, evidence level), 5 ValueSets, 9 StructureDefinitions (5 profiles + 4 extensions), 3 Questionnaires (risk input, facility tier, patient track), and 5 examples plus narrative pages on background, risk assessment workflow, and tier/track implementation. Synthetic example data only; no PHI. For professional use only; not a medical device, not FDA-cleared, not HIPAA-certified.
notes: Disclaimer: This Implementation Guide is a clinical decision support specification for healthcare professionals. It does not provide medical diagnoses, treatment recommendations for individual patients, or replace clinical judgment. Not intended for direct patient care. For professional use only. The HEARTLAND Risk Stratification Framework is a proposed tool under development; formal validation against clinical outcomes data is a defined research objective.
